Description.Text: AWS Cost & Usage Report (CUR) data at PER-RESOURCE granularity - includes resource_id. Use this dataset whenever a question involves specific resources (which resource_id drives cost, tagging/ownership by resource, top resources by spend). Daily granularity (usage_date), rolling ~30-day window, and only rows that have a resource_id. For historical trends beyond 30 days or aggregate analysis without resource detail, use summary_view instead.

InlineCustomInstruction.InstructionText:
FIELD NAMES
All field names in these instructions are the dataset (QuickSight/SPICE) field names - reference them exactly as written. In the CUR COLUMN MAPPING below, the format is `dataset_field -> source CUR column`: the right side is the original AWS Cost and Usage Report column, shown so you can apply standard CUR knowledge. Query using the dataset_field (left side), not the CUR column.

WHEN TO USE
Use resource_view for any per-resource question: cost or usage by resource_id, identifying the most expensive resources, resource-level tagging/attribution. This is the ONLY view with resource_id. Window is a rolling ~30 days at DAILY granularity; rows without a resource_id are excluded. For longer history or resource-agnostic aggregates, use summary_view.

COST COLUMNS
- unblended_cost: raw line-item cost (default metric for this view).
- reservation_effective_cost / savings_plan_effective_cost: effective cost for RI-covered and SP-covered usage respectively. For an amortized/effective view, use these where present and fall back to unblended_cost for on-demand.
- No public_cost, amortized_cost, or charge_category columns here - use summary_view for those.

RI/SP
reservation_a_r_n / savings_plan_a_r_n: ARN of the covering RI or Savings Plan (empty if none). pricing_term reflects the pricing model.

CUR COLUMN MAPPING
usage_date -> line_item_usage_start_date (daily)
resource_id -> line_item_resource_id
payer_account_id -> bill_payer_account_id
linked_account_id -> line_item_usage_account_id
product_code -> line_item_product_code
service -> product_servicecode (friendly name)
product_name -> product_product_name
operation -> line_item_operation
charge_type -> line_item_line_item_type
usage_type -> line_item_usage_type
item_description -> line_item_line_item_description
pricing_unit -> pricing_unit
pricing_term -> pricing_term
region -> product_region
instance_type -> product_instance_type
instance_type_family -> product_instance_type_family
platform -> product_operating_system
database_engine -> product_database_engine
product_family -> product_product_family
product_group -> product_group
product_deployment_option -> product_deployment_option
product_storage -> product_storage
product_volume_api_name -> product_volume_api_name
product_from_location / product_to_location -> product_from_location, product_to_location
legal_entity -> line_item_legal_entity
billing_entity -> bill_billing_entity

Cost sources: unblended_cost -> line_item_unblended_cost; usage_quantity -> line_item_usage_amount; reservation_effective_cost -> reservation_effective_cost; savings_plan_effective_cost -> savings_plan_savings_plan_effective_cost. reservation_a_r_n -> reservation_reservation_a_r_n; savings_plan_a_r_n -> savings_plan_savings_plan_a_r_n.

USAGE & ACCOUNTS
usage_quantity: consumed quantity in pricing_unit.
account_id / account_name: friendly account identifiers (may be NULL if mapping not configured).

ORGANIZATIONAL TAXONOMY (may be present)
Depending on the CUR and this deployment's configuration, additional columns for organizational taxonomy may be present - user-defined cost allocation tags (typically prefixed resource_tags_ / user_) and AWS Cost Categories (typically prefixed cost_category_). When present, use them to group cost by organizational dimensions such as team, environment, cost center, project, or business unit (chargeback/showback). Inspect available columns at query time; do not assume a fixed set.

QUERY TIPS
- Top resources: GROUP BY resource_id ORDER BY SUM(unblended_cost) DESC.
- Resource within a service/account: filter service / linked_account_id, GROUP BY resource_id.
- Keep the time filter tight - only ~30 days are available.
